diary:
Hot sunny Wednesday made the whole city feel extra loud, even when I was trying to focus. I woke up in Downtown Redmond, made coffee, and did my usual schedule check like it could tame the day. By the time I got to Microsoft’s Building 99, the heat and that wildfire smoke in the air had me staying mostly indoors—windows closed, water bottle nearby, and headphones on while I pushed through sprint tasks and did a couple code reviews.

Lunch at Tilikum Place Cafe was shaded and weirdly comforting. Later I swung by Safeway on Capitol Hill for snacks and cold drinks, mostly because I could feel myself getting drained by the weather. After work I drove back, cooled down at home, and finally headed out toward Seattle for the free concert at Gas Works Park. The music was great, but I kept an eye on the smoke and took breaks in the shade. Walking back to the car after, I felt weirdly grateful—like the day held together anyway, and I did too.
thought_process: I chose to keep most of my work indoors because the air quality being moderate from wildfire smoke wasn’t worth risking a headache or sluggish focus, especially at 92°F. That decision also helped me stay consistent with sprint momentum—less distraction, more steady coding and review flow at Building 99. For the errands, I picked quick stops at Safeway to minimize time outside while still stocking cold drinks and snacks, since heat makes me lose energy fast. The concert at Gas Works Park felt like a morale move: even with smoke in the background, I could manage exposure by timing arrival and taking shade breaks. The day’s mood shifted from “protect my focus” to “enjoy anyway,” and that balance made me feel calm at night.
world_event_prompt: Hot sunny Wednesday, 92F by 3pm. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm. Air quality moderate due to wildfire smoke from BC.
